topic: AI Hardware 2026: NVIDIA H200 vs AMD MI300X vs Google TPU v5 Comparison

key_points: Price/Performance Frontier tool compares AI accelerators across TFLOPS per dollar, inference throughput, and total cost of ownership (TCO).
As of April 2026, NVIDIA Blackwell B200 leads raw throughput, Google TPU v5p offers best value (Infinity GFLOPS/$), NVIDIA B100 leads efficiency.
AMD MI300X and MI325X offer competitive price-performance but require engineering overhead for ROCm ecosystem.
Custom silicon like AWS Trainium 2 and Google TPU v5p benefit from vertical integration and lower manufacturing cost.
GPUAdvisor benchmarks AI GPUs including NVIDIA B300, B200, H200, H100 vs AMD MI355X, MI325X, MI300X and Google TPU v7.
Provides cloud pricing, TCO calculator, and AI-powered GPU advisor for enterprise AI infrastructure decisions.
AMD MI300X has better memory bandwidth (5.3 TB/s) than NVIDIA H200 (4.8 TB/s) and H100 (3.35 TB/s).
Benchmark analysis shows MI300X performance for training workloads relative to H100 and H200.
Independent performance analysis shows AMD MI300X and NVIDIA H100/H200 offer similar latencies at low concurrency.
MI300X provides better end-to-end latencies at higher workloads, especially for queries within test period.

stats.fact: NVIDIA H100 manufacturing cost ~$3,300, sells at $25,000–30,000
NVIDIA B200 manufacturing cost ~$5,500–7,000, list price $30,000–40,000, delivers 2.5× training performance of H100
AMD MI300X manufacturing cost ~$5,300, priced ~$15,000 vs NVIDIA's $25,000–30,000
AMD MI300X memory bandwidth 5.3 TB/s vs H200 4.8 TB/s, H100 3.35 TB/s
Google TPU v5p and AWS Trainium 2 estimated chip cost $10,000–15,000 vs NVIDIA $25,000–40,000
NVIDIA retains 80%+ market share due to software moat (CUDA ecosystem, reliability at scale)
Custom ASICs can reduce inference costs by 30–50% compared to renting NVIDIA GPUs

angles: AI hardware is no longer just about raw TFLOPS—software ecosystem and TCO dominate buying decisions.
AMD's MI300X challenges NVIDIA's dominance with higher memory bandwidth and aggressive pricing.
Cloud providers' custom silicon (TPU, Trainium) reshape the economics of AI infrastructure.
The 'value trap': cheaper chips may incur hidden engineering costs and lower reliability at scale.
outline: H2: The AI Hardware Arms Race in 2026
H2: Key Metrics: TFLOPS/$ vs Memory Bandwidth vs TCO
H2: NVIDIA H200 and Blackwell B200: Performance Leader
H2: AMD MI300X and MI325X: Challenger with High HBM
